rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical [3+2] annulation of 3-aza-1,5-dienes with dimethyl malonate to access 4-pyrrolin-2-ones with cathodic H2 evolution
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation generates a malonyl radical from deprotonated dimethyl malonate; cathodic reduction of protons produces H2

key_intermediates.name: malonyl-stabilized C-centered radical from dimethyl malonate
N-acyl alpha-amino allyl/C-centered radical after radical addition to the methacrylamide alkene
cyclized N-acyl iminium / radical cation intermediate prior to deprotonation/aromatization to 4-pyrrolin-2-one

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes the malonate anion (formed by n-Bu4NOAc base) to a malonyl C-radical that adds to the methacrylamide C=C of the 3-aza-1,5-diene; the resulting carbon radical cyclizes onto the pendant styrenyl alkene, and a further SET oxidation/deprotonation furnishes the 4-pyrrolin-2-one, with protons reduced at the stainless-steel cathode to H2.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO/BHT (radical quenching experiments)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction suppressed in the presence of radical scavengers, supporting a radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: electricity on/off
probe_reagents.observation: Product formation only progresses while current is applied, indicating continuous electrolysis is required

probe_reagents.name: H2 detection (GC)
probe_reagents.observation: H2 detected as the cathodic counter-product

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of substrates/malonate
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation event consistent with anodic oxidation of the malonate anion being the initiating SET step

notes: Mechanism inferred from the SI section headings (quenching, on/off, H2 detection, CV) and the gram-scale electrolysis scheme using C(+)/stainless-steel(-) electrodes with n-Bu4NOAc in MeCN; full mechanistic schemes were not visible on the supplied pages, so specific intermediate SMILES beyond the malonyl radical are left null.
